description_long: This object is a long, slender sword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade is uniform in width and appears to be forged from a dark metallic material, giving it a matte, almost black finish. The crossguard is simple and straight, extending horizontally to provide hand protection, and is also made of the same dark metal. The handle is cylindrical and elongated, featuring subtle ridges for grip, and ends in a rounded pommel. There are no visible engravings, inlays, or decorative elements on the blade, guard, or handle, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall appearance suggests a functional weapon, possibly inspired by medieval European longswords.
description: A straight, double-edged sword with a dark metallic blade, simple crossguard, and a ridged cylindrical handle ending in a rounded pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and the rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and pommel from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, ridged, dark metal, rounded pommel
